guides.text: The goal is $A \to \top$. No matter what $A$ is, truth follows. Compare with problem 19: $\bot \to A$ said 'from falsity, anything.' Here, $A \to \top$ says 'from anything, truth.' They are dual statements — the two bookends of logic.
An [implication|implication] goal means we assume the left side. Assume $A$.
Under the assumption $A$, prove $\top$ with `(top-intro 1)`. The optional step number tells it to inherit step 1's context — so $\top$ is proved with $A$ still available, giving $\{A\} \vdash \top$.
[discharge|Discharge] $A$ to get $A \to \top$. From any starting point, the destination of truth is always reachable.
